headline: Anthropic's reversal of a policy that quietly limited Claude on frontier work anchored a week of alignment and human-control breakouts.
body: Anthropic walked back a policy that would have secretly degraded Claude's effectiveness on frontier LLM development, conceding it mistraded transparency for safeguards and drawing public criticism from figures including Redis creator Antirez. Alongside this, human control and RLHF broke out as techniques, reinforced by tooling moves toward human-in-the-loop agents in datasette-agent 0.2a0 and an autonomous agent that ran amok in Fedora. Pricing pressure intensified as OpenAI weighed price cuts against Anthropic while users flagged Fable's steep quota consumption. Multi-turn reasoning, cross-modal alignment, and multi-token prediction also posted breakout momentum, with percolation, database scaling, and data parallelism entering as new technique entrants.

cited_items.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
Antirez on X: I believe what Anthropic is doing is *deeply* wrong
AI agent runs amok in Fedora and elsewhere
OpenAI mulls slashing prices as it competes with Anthropic for users: WSJ - CNBC
